(or Offensive Debuffs These debuffs will almost always be present on Raid bosses. Without Judgement of Wisdom, sustaining your mana for an entire fight can be an issue. Improved Shadow Bolt is supplied by Warlocks; charges are consumed by direct damage, but the damage bonus applies to all shadow damage regardless. The majority of your spells do not consume charges, but do massively benefit from the damage boost, so this debuff is invaluable for Shadow Priests. Buff Effect Increased shadow damage taken Increased spell damage taken / Reduced resistance & increased damage taken by most schools Increased shadow damage taken Increased critical strike chance Spells have a chance to restore mana Ideal Raid Group Setup Shadow Priests bring fantastic utility that enables Arcane Mages by providing them with a significant boost in Mana that is almost required for Arcane to work. You always want a Shaman in your group, and for Shadow Priests and Arcane Mages, it is usually not very important if they are Restoration or Elemental; the difference being the specialized totem they provide. Protection Paladin is a natural filler in this group as the extra mana allows them to consistently cast even while not taking damage, and they are interested in the same totems as the rest of the group. If you run 3 Arcane Mages, prioritize them over the Paladin. Some groups opt for Healers in the Shadow Priest group instead, but this is rarely necessary with how quick most fights are and how plentiful mana can be from consumables. Shadow Priest (you) Elemental or Restoration Shaman - or Protection Paladin Arcane Mage Arcane Mage Best Consumables for Shadow Priest DPS in TBC Classic Consumables are vital for all classes and specializations, Shadow Priests being no exception! Best Elixirs for Shadow Priest DPS In TBC Classic, you can have one Battle Elixir and one Guardian Elixir at any one time. Generally, Shadow Priests avoid using Elixirs as they are significantly worse than their flask option. If you are trying to save gold or are unable to get flasks, the combination of Elixir of Major Shadow Power and Elixir of Major Mageblood grants a decent amount of Shadow Power as well as a chunk of Mana regeneration, potentially helping you on longer fights where Mana is a concern. Best Flask for Shadow Priest DPS Flask of Pure Death is the best flask for Shadow Priests, increasing shadow spell damage by 80 for 2 hours. This is quite a big improvement over elixirs, so there are few reasons to use anything else. Best Weapon Enhancement for Shadow Priest DPS Another lucky break for Shadow Priests, Superior Wizard Oil is generally the cheapest Weapon Oil available, whilst also being the best option for us. Best Chestplate Enhancement for Shadow Priest DPS Due to it being the only temporary chestplate imbue, Greater Rune of Warding is the best choice here. It provides you with a 25% chance of absorbing 400 physical damage when hit, which is always useful to have, especially since it's fairly cheap to make. Best Food for Shadow Priest DPS Your choice in food is fairly straightforward: Blackened Basilisk , Crunchy Serpent , or Poached Bluefish . All 3 of these provide the same bonus, and there are no real substitutes. Best Potion for Shadow Priest DPS Your choice of Potion depends entirely on the fight. If you can get through an entire fight without needing a Mana Potion, you always want to use Destruction Potion to boost your damage significantly. Ideally, you want to combine Destruction Potions with an on-use Trinket such as Icon of the Silver Crescent and a fresh round of DoTs! If you need Mana, which is typical in AoE and Council style fights, Super Mana Potion comes in several flavours. Mana Potion Injector is made with Engineering, which can stack up to 20 and save you a lot of space. Auchenai Mana Potion is effectively identical, just available from different sources. Cenarion Mana Salve and Bottled Nethergon Energy each stack up to 15 and provide the same Mana as the other potions listed, but are only usable in their respective areas. Best Secondary Combat Consumables for Shadow Priest DPS Dark Rune and Demonic Rune are invaluable. These items cost a small amount of health for a decent amount of mana, but do not share cooldowns with Potions, instead sharing cooldowns with Master Healthstone . Because of this, you can use these freely to significantly improve your Mana during a fight. Best Scrolls for Shadow Priest DPS There's little reason to use scrolls as a Shadow Priest unless you're completely missing a buff, such as Scroll of Intellect V or Scroll of Versatility V . Scroll of Protection V gives a little extra armor, which can be useful occasionally. Best Miscellaneous Consumables for Shadow Priest DPS Kreeg's Stout Beatdown is worth considering if you bring a Priest with , as this stacks with your food buff. Without it, this is such a tiny effect that it is typically not worth worrying about. Even though you can heal, having and using Heavy Netherweave Bandage is also helpful for moments when you need to heal yourself. Purified Draenic Water is your go-to Water, and you should always carry a large amount of Water with you, but alternatives exist. Mages can conjure Conjured Glacier Water , but this generally takes a lot of time and Mana if you want to supply your entire raid. Star's Tears is usable in Arena. I always make sure to have 200 Black Coffee in my bags at all times. This Water is identical to the others, but who doesn't prefer a nice cup of coffee? Finally, Kibler's Bits can be fed to your Shadowfiend on summon in order to increase its damage slightly. The amount of Damage is mostly negligible, but all Shadowfiend damage converts directly to more Mana for you! Best Event Consumables for Shadow Priest DPS While it's a more uncommon type of consumable, Snowball s can provide a ridiculous amount of MP5 when macro'd into your instant cast spells, as they can proc . Since they're only available during the Winter Veil event, you need to stock up on a lot of Snowballs if you wish to keep using them throughout the year. Midsummer Sausage and Very Berry Cream stack with your regular food buff, which means they're always worth using. Sadly, the real-time timer on them limits their usefulness quite a bit, as they are only available during specific times of the year and cannot be saved for progression raids. Graccu's Mince Meat Fruitcake , Festival Dumplings , and Candy Bar (different variations) are all event-related items that are sought after because they are the only foods that restore a percentage of your mana. By combining any of them with regular water, you can fill up your entire mana bar incredibly quickly. Best Profession Consumables for Shadow Priest DPS in TBC Classic If you really want to take the best to your raids, you can also choose your professions with the best available consumables in mind. Engineering Items Super Sapper Charge is a fantastic addition to your kit, providing you with a much-needed option for AoE damage, even if it can be pricey at times. Goblin Sapper Charge is a lesser version of the Super Sapper Charge, but it is not on the same 5-minute cooldown; instead, they each put the other on a shorter cooldown, meaning you can rotate between the two. Fel Iron Bomb , Adamantite Grenade , and Arcane Bomb are all on a shared CD with each other, but are not on the same 5-minute cooldown as Sappers, meaning you can rotate these in the mix as well. Which one you use is situational. Bombs cannot be used while moving, but have a large radius, while Grenades can be used while moving, but they deal damage in a smaller area. For more details about Engineering in The Burning Crusade Classic, please see our guide below. Engineering Overview Guide Leatherworking Items Drums of Battle will typically be used by your group's Shaman, and should rarely be a worry for you. They are a very short-range buff for the user's party. Everyone affected is debuffed with Tinnitus and is unable to benefit from Drums again for 2 minutes. Because of this, you only need one person in your group with Leatherworking, and ideally it won't be you. Leatherworking Overview Guide

Best Chestplate Enhancement for Shadow Priest DPS Due to it being the only temporary chestplate imbue, Greater Rune of Warding is the best choice here. It provides you with a 25% chance of absorbing 400 physical damage when hit, which is always useful to have, especially since it's fairly cheap to make. Best Food for Shadow Priest DPS Your choice in food is fairly straightforward: Blackened Basilisk , Crunchy Serpent , or Poached Bluefish . All 3 of these provide the same bonus, and there are no real substitutes. Best Potion for Shadow Priest DPS Your choice of Potion depends entirely on the fight. If you can get through an entire fight without needing a Mana Potion, you always want to use Destruction Potion to boost your damage significantly. Ideally, you want to combine Destruction Potions with an on-use Trinket such as Icon of the Silver Crescent and a fresh round of DoTs! If you need Mana, which is typical in AoE and Council style fights, Super Mana Potion comes in several flavours. Mana Potion Injector is made with Engineering, which can stack up to 20 and save you a lot of space. Auchenai Mana Potion is effectively identical, just available from different sources. Cenarion Mana Salve and Bottled Nethergon Energy each stack up to 15 and provide the same Mana as the other potions listed, but are only usable in their respective areas. Best Secondary Combat Consumables for Shadow Priest DPS Dark Rune and Demonic Rune are invaluable. These items cost a small amount of health for a decent amount of mana, but do not share cooldowns with Potions, instead sharing cooldowns with Master Healthstone . Because of this, you can use these freely to significantly improve your Mana during a fight. Best Scrolls for Shadow Priest DPS There's little reason to use scrolls as a Shadow Priest unless you're completely missing a buff, such as Scroll of Intellect V or Scroll of Versatility V . Scroll of Protection V gives a little extra armor, which can be useful occasionally. Best Miscellaneous Consumables for Shadow Priest DPS Kreeg's Stout Beatdown is worth considering if you bring a Priest with , as this stacks with your food buff. Without it, this is such a tiny effect that it is typically not worth worrying about. Even though you can heal, having and using Heavy Netherweave Bandage is also helpful for moments when you need to heal yourself. Purified Draenic Water is your go-to Water, and you should always carry a large amount of Water with you, but alternatives exist. Mages can conjure Conjured Glacier Water , but this generally takes a lot of time and Mana if you want to supply your entire raid. Star's Tears is usable in Arena. I always make sure to have 200 Black Coffee in my bags at all times. This Water is identical to the others, but who doesn't prefer a nice cup of coffee? Finally, Kibler's Bits can be fed to your Shadowfiend on summon in order to increase its damage slightly. The amount of Damage is mostly negligible, but all Shadowfiend damage converts directly to more Mana for you! Best Event Consumables for Shadow Priest DPS While it's a more uncommon type of consumable, Snowball s can provide a ridiculous amount of MP5 when macro'd into your instant cast spells, as they can proc . Since they're only available during the Winter Veil event, you need to stock up on a lot of Snowballs if you wish to keep using them throughout the year. Midsummer Sausage and Very Berry Cream stack with your regular food buff, which means they're always worth using. Sadly, the real-time timer on them limits their usefulness quite a bit, as they are only available during specific times of the year and cannot be saved for progression raids. Graccu's Mince Meat Fruitcake , Festival Dumplings , and Candy Bar (different variations) are all event-related items that are sought after because they are the only foods that restore a percentage of your mana. By combining any of them with regular water, you can fill up your entire mana bar incredibly quickly. Best Profession Consumables for Shadow Priest DPS in TBC Classic If you really want to take the best to your raids, you can also choose your professions with the best available consumables in mind. Engineering Items Super Sapper Charge is a fantastic addition to your kit, providing you with a much-needed option for AoE damage, even if it can be pricey at times. Goblin Sapper Charge is a lesser version of the Super Sapper Charge, but it is not on the same 5-minute cooldown; instead, they each put the other on a shorter cooldown, meaning you can rotate between the two. Fel Iron Bomb , Adamantite Grenade , and Arcane Bomb are all on a shared CD with each other, but are not on the same 5-minute cooldown as Sappers, meaning you can rotate these in the mix as well. Which one you use is situational. Bombs cannot be used while moving, but have a large radius, while Grenades can be used while moving, but they deal damage in a smaller area. For more details about Engineering in The Burning Crusade Classic, please see our guide below. Engineering Overview Guide Leatherworking Items Drums of Battle will typically be used by your group's Shaman, and should rarely be a worry for you. They are a very short-range buff for the user's party. Everyone affected is debuffed with Tinnitus and is unable to benefit from Drums again for 2 minutes. Because of this, you only need one person in your group with Leatherworking, and ideally it won't be you. Leatherworking Overview Guide
